operating_state: Total revenue increased 6% to $57,799 driven by 4% comparable sales growth and 25 net new warehouses opened since prior year quarter. Core merchandise categories led growth with fresh foods and foods and sundries driving performance while warehouse ancillary businesses increased 3%. Membership fee revenue increased 8% driven by new member sign-ups, Executive membership upgrades, and higher renewal rates reaching 92.8% in U.S. and Canada. Operating cash flows increased to $4,651 million from $2,610 million driven by strong sales performance and membership fee collections.
cost_structure: Gross margin percentage increased 43 basis points to 11.04% driven by warehouse ancillary businesses improvement and absence of prior year charter shipping discontinuation charge. Core merchandise gross margin increased 5 basis points with non-foods improvement partially offset by fresh foods pressure. SG&A expenses as percentage of net sales increased 25 basis points to 9.45% primarily due to warehouse operations cost increases including wage increases implemented in March and September 2023. Effective tax rate rose to 24.5% from 23.0%.
material_events: Craig Jelinek announced intention to step down as CEO effective January 1, 2024, with Ron Vachris elected as President and CEO. Board declared quarterly cash dividend of $1.02 per share and subsequent special cash dividend of $15.00 per share payable January 12, 2024, totaling approximately $6.7 billion. Japan subsidiary issued four Guaranteed Senior Notes totaling approximately $500 million with maturities from November 2033 to November 2043.
macro_exposures: Lower gasoline prices negatively impacted net sales by $341 or 64 basis points with 4% decrease in average price per gallon. Foreign currency changes positively impacted net sales by approximately $195 or 36 basis points from Other International operations partially offset by Canadian operations. Government tariff actions particularly between China and United States continue to affect merchandise costs with exposure dependent on goods type and timing.
forward_outlook: Management continues focus on increasing comparable sales as primary profitability driver through maintaining pricing authority and competitive values for members. Strategy emphasizes controlling costs while maintaining above-industry-average compensation levels to reduce employee turnover. Capital expenditures of $4,400 to $4,600 planned for fiscal 2024 primarily for new warehouse development and information systems, financed through operating cash flows and existing cash balances.
uncertainty_profile: Multiple employment-related class action lawsuits filed in California, New York, and New Jersey alleging wage and hour violations with immaterial accruals recorded for certain matters. Ongoing opioid litigation consolidated in federal MDL with claims from various jurisdictions and Civil Investigative Demand received from U.S. Attorney's Office regarding False Claims Act investigation concerning prescription medications. Privacy law violation class actions filed regarding pixel trackers on Costco.com website.
